threat_actor.name: SCARLETEEL
threat_actor.motivation: Financial — cryptomining revenue via resource hijacking; intellectual property theft (Lambda source code, environment variables); lateral movement for expanded access

credential_chain.source: IMDSv1 credential theft from inside vulnerable Docker container via command injection in Flask app
credential_chain.credential_type: AWS EC2 instance role temporary credentials (AssumedRole)
credential_chain.expiration: short-term (~6 hours, not renewable from attacker perspective)

credential_chain.source: terraform.tfstate file stored in S3 bucket scarleteel-tf-state-*
credential_chain.credential_type: AWS IAM long-term access key (AKIA*) for zero-permission bait user

ip_addresses: 223.233.84.57 (attacker source IP observed in emulation run)

attack_creates_resources: XMRig miner files in /tmp/ on compromised container host
IAM CreateUser attempt (ScarleteelBackdoor) — expected to fail via role policy
IAM CreateAccessKey attempt (AdminJoe) — expected to fail via role policy

cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Running a real cryptominer consumes compute resources and may violate AWS ToS
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Deploy XMRig configuration files and a mock shell script that prints simulated output

cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Sending credentials to real attacker infrastructure is illegal and harmful
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Log the exfiltration command to /tmp/exfil_sim.log with comment explaining the real technique
